location.office_time_required: some in-person meetings; primarily flexibility to work from home

level_signals: 4+ years of experience in product operations, growth, go-to-market, strategy and operations, or analytics roles
Compensation range $92,293 - $131,847 base consistent with senior IC contributor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of experience in product operations, growth, go-to-market, strategy and operations, or analytics roles
You have owned or contributed to measurable business outcomes, such as revenue growth, conversion, or pipeline development
You are comfortable working with AI tools and have experience prompting, evaluating, and improving LLM-based workflows
You know how to use SQL and BigQuery to track performance, assess data quality, and identify issues early
You have experience working in Salesforce, including configuring workflows, understanding data models, or building automation
You take a thoughtful, structured approach to quality and are comfortable working with testing, validation, and release processes
You communicate clearly and collaborate well with cross-functional partners to move work forward

scope.surface: Revenue Growth team — advertiser lifecycle programs, go-to-market execution, Salesforce automation
core_responsibilities: Work on programs that support revenue growth and improve how advertisers engage and succeed at scale
Build and improve lifecycle programs that drive advertiser activation, retention, and revenue, using data to guide decisions and iterate over time
Turn team briefs into fully functioning programs in Salesforce, including logic, routing, response handling, and integrations
Take ownership of program delivery end-to-end, ensuring each launch is stable, scalable, and ready for new audiences
Maintain a high bar for quality by testing outputs, applying clear evaluation criteria, and refining programs before launch
Design and run experiments to improve performance, using results to adjust program logic and drive better outcomes
Monitor program health using BigQuery, identifying patterns or issues early and taking action to keep systems running smoothly

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($92,293 - $131,847 base) plus equity
Flexible hybrid model with remote-first option in NYC timezone
Concrete technical skill requirements (SQL, BigQuery, Salesforce) make expectations clear
Strong benefits package including 6-month paid parental leave and 23 paid days off
Clear team mission and program scope described
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
LLM/AI tool requirement is somewhat vague — no clarity on depth expected
No explicit mention of growth path or team structure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from compensation range and 4+ years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated directly)
scope.business_model (inferred from ad sales and advertiser focus)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'flexibility to work where you work best' language)
ambiguities: Location type: JD says 'ideally based in New York City' with flexibility to work from home; treated as hybrid with remote flexibility rather than strictly onsite.
Nice-to-haves section is absent — all requirements listed under 'Who You Are' are treated as hard must-haves since no preferred/bonus language is used.
